target: B7: Regularly-invoked protocols compound system quality over time
falsification_hypothesis: PCI (compliance) rises while science_quality (substance) stays flat. Correlation < 0.3. B7 conflates protocol field-filling with knowledge quality.

compliance_trajectory.verdict: RAPID COMPOUNDING — binary jump at enforcement point

expectations_vs_actual.correlation_verdict: FALSIFICATION FAILS — SciQ does increase, not flat
expectations_vs_actual.productivity_actual: L/session peaked before EAD, declined 23% after

verdict: B7 CONFIRMED — falsification attempt FAILS. Protocols DO compound quality (SciQ +6.4x post-EAD). But 3 qualifications: (1) compliance compounds 4x faster than quality (70pp gap), (2) L/session declined 23% post-enforcement, (3) enforcement is binary jump not gradual compounding. B7 is directionally correct but magnitude-misleading.
qualifications: Q1: Compliance compounds 4x faster than science quality (70pp PCI-SciQ gap at current scale)
Q2: Productivity (L/session) peaked at 5.77 pre-enforcement, declined to 4.41 post-enforcement (-23%)
Q3: Post-EAD SciQ acceleration 6.4x provides strongest evidence compliance IS causal for quality gains
lesson_candidate: B7 compliance-quality gap: protocol compliance compounds rapidly (0→97%), science quality compounds slowly (0.019→0.247, 13x but still 25%). Post-enforcement SciQ acceleration 6.4x is the strongest evidence B7 is correct. But productivity declined 23%. Compliance≠quality: the metric gap (70pp) means B7's quality claim is self-referential unless measured with external quality metrics.
